ab: akuku maimi mana, maruaru amu rʉhlʉcʉ, mulavaʉ umaiiapʉ, ahlausuhlu cu a Takau, rianʉ ma ikatumahlana mangusipi.
ch: 小時候我們家住在高中，上學以後，就搬到了高雄，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Haising. After I started school, we moved to Kaohsiung and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: ma cu ariataisa mulavaʉ, tiapili a ku ucani siatuturu a apulavaʉ vunguka isa cucumacu, macahli tarʉtatʉkʉhlʉ siaravacangʉ vunguka isa Taivangʉ cucumacu.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: kuhlaisai, taluamacicia mamisa, ihlaku nua ahlalua hlahlusa puaili miararuma takuliliungu hlamama, hlatumua macacahlia tahlana vunguka takacicihli.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: malʉhlʉ caihli cu a hlamamana, hla kitaʉia maraiahlʉ mana tamalʉngalʉ .
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: pakisuahlai ku kana’ana tainini macahliisa kari, tam masivacangʉ kari Hla’alua, macahlia mʉmʉa masi kari sumukuhlu, kari puutu hla kari ’aumu.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的拉阿魯哇語說得很流利，也會說布農語、客家話和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Hla’alua, as well as Atayal, Hakka and Japanese.

ab: hlipakisuahlai ku kana’ana iniciki macahlia, usuanganaiau hlimusuturu kari ’aumu akuku mainimana, usumanʉisa ia cucu tahlana sumukuhlu ma ta’iaraisana tumahlaʉ pautuahlangʉisa cucupuutu, kuhlaisai matumuhlu a acahlisa kari.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是布農族人，而他工作上認識許多客家人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Bunun, and he knows many Hakkas at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: akuku takuliliungu amu, hlimakakari hlamamana tam tumuhlu ausikan siaracahlia isa mʉrʉcʉka.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: maniki a mu kutuahlʉ rianʉ a timatakahlangʉ, hla maru a mu uruamia ’arivungua sipiakiatitipa, acahli maiatuhluhlu tumimahla.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: akuku mainimana kuaku macahlia tahlana vunguka takacicihli, marinu aku mahlikua cucumacu.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: matumuhlu siaracahlia rumahlaʉ tumacacurunucikia mara cucu tahlana Hla’alua, pakaritʉkʉhlʉcikia apausakisakiricikia.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為拉阿魯哇族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Hla’alua is unique and something to be proud of.
